# WI-QUIC-API-0005 - Incursa.Quic Pending Outbound Stream Open Work Item

## Summary

Implement pending outbound stream opens that remain queued when the peer limit is exhausted and resume only after later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth makes the requested direction available.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-API-0014

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-API-0005

## Planned Changes

- Track pending outbound open requests by request ID and requested direction so blocked opens can stay queued instead of failing fast.
- Retry queued opens only when a real `MAX_STREAMS` frame raises the matching peer limit.
- Preserve cancellation and terminal-state completion semantics so a queued open cannot be revived after it has been settled.
- Add focused requirement-home proof for pending behavior, later real `MAX_STREAMS` wake-up, and cancellation while blocked.

## Out of Scope

- `STREAMS_BLOCKED` emission as a public API completion mechanism.
- Broader stream-management parity.
- New public API surface.
- 0-RTT.
- Key update.
- Transfer or retry changes.

## Verification Plan

Run the new `REQ-QUIC-API-0014` requirement-home proof plus the existing `REQ-QUIC-API-0008` and `REQ-QUIC-API-0009` regression coverage, then run `git diff --check` and the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts.

## Completion Notes

This slice keeps the blocked open request honest by staying pending until the peer really grows the matching stream limit. It does not make advisory `STREAMS_BLOCKED` emission a completion mechanism; RFC 9000 runtime signaling is owned separately.
